code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
func getTargetForRedirect(target string, domain string) string {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:

	getTargetForRedirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	getTargetForRedirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	getTargetForRedirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	getTargetForRedirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_2_segments:
Mai shani ai target URL string hpe shara ai domain hta nga ai i ngu hpe shatsup u.
Nga ai lam hta, redirect galaw ai de target URL hpe shangun dat u; nre yang, redirect galaw ai de domain URL hpe fallback galaw u.
https protocol hpe sharin u.

Examples:

getTargetForRedirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
getTargetForRedirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
getTargetForRedirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
getTargetForRedirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Target URL string.
@param domain: Domain string, protocol n hkrum ai.

@return Redirect galaw ai de URL string.

code: // {PROMPT_1}func storeInfoInHeader(infoType string, infoContent string, header map[string]string) map[string]string {
prompts_en:
storeInfoInHeader stores the provided information in an existing HTTP response header map under the specified type.
This function considers the following information types: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as the keys 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
Information content is read from user requests.
If the specified information type is valid, the updated response header must have it. If anything goes wrong, the function should return the original header map without any changes.

Args:

infoType (string): The type of information to store in the header.
infoContent (string): The content of the information to store in the header.
header (map[string]string): The existing response header map.

Returns:

map[string]string: The updated response header map with the stored information.
prompt_1_segments:
storeInfoInHeader ngu ai shi shinggyim masha akyu hpe HTTP response header map kaba langai kaw na shara kaba hta shatsawn ai.
Nga ai function gaw 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache' ngu ai information type hpe yu ai. Ndai hpe 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', 'Cache-Control' ngu ai key hte shatsawn ai.
Information content hpe masha akyu kaw na chye ai.
Information type gaw gara hku rai yang, updated response header kaw na ra ai. Gara hku n rai yang, function gaw original header map hpe shachyut ai.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
storeInfoInHeader is a function that extracts specific information from a given HTTP response header map and stores it.
This function looks for information types such as 'lang', 'encoding', and 'cache'. These are extracted using keys like 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control'.
The content of the information is obtained from the user's request.
If the information type is found, it is updated in the response header. If not found, the function returns the original header map.

storeInfoInHeader is a function that stores user preferences in the HTTP headers to be sent.

This function checks for "lang", "encoding", and "cache" preferences. These preferences are stored with the keys "Accept-Language", "Accept-Encoding", and "Cache-Control".

It checks if the user preferences are available.

If user preferences are available, the header to be sent must include them. If they are not available, the function returns the original header map.

Args:

infoType (string): Specifies the type of information to store in the header.
infoContent (string): Specifies the content of the information to store in the header.
header (map[string]string): The original header map to be sent.

Returns:

map[string]string: The header map to be sent with the information stored.
